document.title: IEC 60601-1 Medical electrical equipment Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance, Report No. 397950
document.documentType: CB Test Report / safety test report
document.revisionOrVersion: Report Reference No. 397950; TRF No. IEC60601_1P; Master TRF 2019-10-11

sourceSummary.oneLine: Nemko Korea CB Scheme safety test report for H&abyz ADD Abyz Digital Detector against IEC 60601-1 and related collateral requirements.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Evidence of basic safety and essential performance testing for a medical electrical digital flat panel X-ray detector in CB Scheme and national-difference contexts.
keyPoints: Test item is described as Abyz Digital Detector with model/type reference ADD.
The product fulfils IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD1:2012 and EN 60601-1:2006 + A1:2013 requirements, with collateral IEC 60601-1-6 evaluation.

Equipment classifications include Class I when connected through the power supply box, internally powered when disconnected from mains, Type B applied part, ordinary IPX0, and continuous operation with intermittent loading.
Risk management was documented on the basis of ISO 14971:2007 and EN ISO 14971:2012 and reviewed by Nemko.
Clause 16 ME system compliance is not covered and must be evaluated in the end-product X-ray system when applicable.
EMC compliance to IEC 60601-1-2 is declared and documented by the manufacturer, not evaluated as a test verdict in this report.

evidence.claim: The report was issued under IEC 60601-1 for medical electrical equipment basic safety and essential performance.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1 Medical electrical equipment Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance

evidence.claim: The report reference number is 397950 and the date of issue is 2020-09-28.
Nemko Korea Co., Ltd. was the CB Testing Laboratory.
evidence.evidenceText: CB Testing Laboratory .................. : Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: H&abyz Co., Ltd. was the applicant.
evidence.evidenceText: Applicant’s name ........................... : H&abyz Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: The tested item was the Abyz Digital Detector with model/type reference ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: Test item description ....................... : Abyz Digital Detector ... Model/Type reference ...................... : ADD

evidence.claim: The test procedure was the CB Scheme.
The applicable IEC 60601-1 test specification was IEC 60601-1:2005 with corrigenda and Amendment 1:2012, or IEC 60601-1:2012 reprint.
The product fulfils IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD1:2012 and EN 60601-1:2006 + A1:2013.
evidence.evidenceText: The product fulfils the requirements of - IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD1:2012 and EN 60601-1:2006 + A1:2013

evidence.claim: The equipment was also evaluated to IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + AMD1:2013.
evidence.evidenceText: The equipment has been evaluated also according to collateral standard IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + AMD1:2013

evidence.claim: Risk management results were documented on ISO 14971:2007 and EN ISO 14971:2012 and reviewed by Nemko.
evidence.evidenceText: RM Results are documented by the manufacturer on the basis of ISO 14971:2007 and EN ISO 14971:2012 ... and are reviewed by Nemko.

evidence.claim: The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children in hospital or institutional healthcare environments.
evidence.evidenceText: The equipment is intended to be indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children used in a hospital or other institutional healthcare environment.

evidence.claim: The equipment is not intended for mammography or direct cardiac application.
evidence.evidenceText: But the equipment is not intended to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application.

evidence.claim: The device is Class I ME equipment when the power supply box is connected to mains and internally powered ME equipment when disconnected from mains.
evidence.evidenceText: Class I ME equipment when the power supply box is connected to the supply mains, or - Internally powered ME equipment when the power box is disconnected to the supply mains

evidence.claim: The front panel of the digital flat panel X-ray detector is a Type B applied part.
evidence.evidenceText: TYPE B APPLIED PART Front panel of the digital flat panel X-ray detector is classified as type B applied part.

evidence.claim: The equipment has ordinary ingress protection classification IPX0.
evidence.evidenceText: Degree of protection against harmful ingress of water or particulate matter: Ordinary (IPX0)

evidence.claim: The mode of operation is continuous operation with intermittent loading.
evidence.evidenceText: Mode of operation: Continuous operation with intermittent loading

evidence.claim: Clause 16 ME system compliance is not covered by this report and must be evaluated in the end-product when applicable.
evidence.evidenceText: This test report does not cover the clause 16. The compliance shall be tested and evaluated in the end-product (X-ray system including operating PC)

evidence.claim: The manufacturer declared compliance with IEC 60601-1-2 for electromagnetic compatibility.
evidence.evidenceText: The manufacturer has declared that the equipment is in compliance with the requirements of IEC 60601-1-2. Compliance is documented by the manufacturer.

evidence.claim: The software life-cycle process was evaluated according to IEC 60601-1 clause 14 and IEC 62304, with software Class B.
evidence.evidenceText: The software life-cycle process has been evaluated according to IEC 60601-1, clause 14 and IEC 62304. ... Software Class: B

evidence.claim: The rechargeable Li-ion battery type SDB-3S1PA was separately tested and certified according to IEC 62133-2:2017.
evidence.evidenceText: The rechargeable Li-ion battery, type SDB-3S1PA has been separately tested and certified by TÜV-Rh (CBTC No. JPTUV-114329) according to IEC 62133-2:2017

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text identifies a CB Scheme/Nemko safety test report and national differences including United States; no FDA document authority or FDA decision is stated in the source text.
Report contains post-issue corrections dated 2020-10-19 for misprints, while the stated date of issue is 2020-09-28.
EMC compliance to IEC 60601-1-2 is documented as a manufacturer declaration with N/E verdict rather than a completed test evaluation in this report.
ME system clause 16 is explicitly not covered and must be evaluated in the end-product X-ray system when applicable.
